channel.title: Projects on Youri Schuurmans
channel.description: Recent content in Projects on Youri Schuurmans

item.title: Alfam
item.description:
As a Junior Consultant assigned to Alfam — a consumer-credit subsidiary of ABN AMRO — I work alongside their internal engineering teams to modernise long-running .NET back-office systems.

My day-to-day involves introducing automated testing into legacy code paths, refactoring tightly-coupled modules into smaller services, and helping the team adopt Azure DevOps pipelines for continuous delivery.

The most rewarding part has been pairing with developers across multiple squads, sharing patterns from other engagements and learning the credit-domain quirks that no textbook covers.

item.title: Boomiu
item.description:
Boomiu is a fast-paced party game built for three or more players. The concept centers on shared tension — players cooperate just enough to keep things going, while simultaneously competing to avoid being the one left holding the bomb when time runs out.

The game was developed as a personal project to explore game design beyond the serious-games context I was working in at the time. I wanted something immediately accessible: no tutorials, no setup friction, playable within seconds of launching.

item.title: Predicting Potential Drug Lab Locations
item.description:
Commissioned by the province of Noord-Brabant, this project explored whether geographic, demographic, and historical-incident data could be combined to predict where illicit drug laboratories were most likely to appear next.

Working in a small team during my Applied Data Science minor, I helped collect and clean dozens of public datasets, engineered features around isolation, road accessibility, and prior incident clusters, and trained gradient-boosted classifiers to score every 500×500 m grid cell in the province.
